name: A & A Limousine
recent_news: No notable recent M&A or news identified; longstanding small Tucson limo/charter operator owned by Gary Aegerter (per SalesSpider listing). Note: a-alimo.com appears to be unrelated Seattle-area A&A; the Tucson entity is at PO Box 50341/2810 E Farr St.
website: unconfirmed (a-alimo.com resolves to Seattle-area A&A, not Tucson entity)
fit_notes: Weak Summit fit: primarily limousine/airport service in Tucson with no clear school-bus or special-needs school-transport contract base; in-state AZ but small scale and limited evidence of student transport make this a low-priority tuck-in.

name: General Transportation
recent_news: No public news identified; website (generaltransportationinc.com) was unreachable (SSL/cert error). Web searches surfaced only general San Jose paratransit info (VTA ACCESS), no specific footprint for this entity.
fit_notes: Insufficient public footprint to evaluate. If confirmed as a small SPED/paratransit operator in San Jose, scale (~25 employees, ~$2M) would be sub-scale for Summit but could be relevant in a CA roll-up; needs direct outreach to verify school-transport contract mix.

name: Empire Medi-Van
principals: Michael Aquino (President, per source record; Mike Aquino noted as retired from Empire Ambulance)
districts_or_customers: Capital Region hospitals/nursing homes (10 hospitals, 25 nursing/LTC facilities served per company description)
recent_news: Affiliated Empire Ambulance sold its ambulance license to DocGo's Ambulnz in Jan 2023, ceasing 911 ambulance service in Cohoes after 30+ years; Empire Medi-Van NEMT/ambulette service remains listed as operating from Cohoes/Clifton Park. Founder Mike Aquino listed as retired.
fit_notes: Mixed Summit fit: NEMT/ambulette focus (not school-bus); however high succession signal (founder retired, ambulance arm divested 2023) and ~100 employees in Capital Region NY could be opportunistic if special-needs school transport is part of the book — worth a screening call.

name: Suburban Transportation Inc
principals: Michael Ilacqua (President, per source record — likely prior generation/family member)
districts_or_customers: Insurance/managed-care payors: Circle Care, UnitedHealthcare, Fidelis Care, Nascentia Health; Central NY school districts using SPED transport (specific districts not disclosed)
recent_news: Mark Ilacqua launched Tobi Cloud (passenger-transport SaaS) in 2021 as co-founder/Head of Product and also runs Highland Patient Transport (2019) — suggests diversified family interests but Suburban continues operating. No announced sale.
fit_notes: Decent Summit fit for NY footprint: Upstate NY (Syracuse HQ with Schenectady ops), explicit SPED school-transport service line, family-owned since 1986. Owner (Mark Ilacqua) is younger/active and building adjacent ventures (Tobi Cloud), so succession likelihood is low near-term but worth relationship-building; ~$3M revenue is small for a tuck-in but adds NEMT/SPED density.

name: Massive Transportation Inc
principals: V J Persaud (Owner, per source record)
service_types: school & employee bus transportation (per directory classification)
districts_or_customers: Mount Vernon CSD uses First Student and All County Bus — Massive/Black Sea is NOT a Mount Vernon CSD primary vendor; likely serves charter/private school/shuttle contracts
recent_news: FMCSA snapshot shows 31 power units, 28 drivers, intrastate-only, last MCS-150 update Aug 2019 (stale); zero crashes/inspections last 24 months. Negative consumer reviews noted on Yelp/Nextdoor under Black Sea Transportation DBA.
website: getmadproducts.com (per source — appears unrelated; no functional company website identified)
fit_notes: Weak Summit fit: ~31 buses/vans in Mount Vernon/Westchester County area with unclear school-district contract base (not on Mount Vernon CSD list), stale FMCSA data, negative consumer reputation under Black Sea DBA. Low priority unless they hold NYC DOE OPT private/charter routes worth confirming.
